name: Red Oak
finish_recommendation: Pre-conditioner recommended before staining to avoid blotching. Takes stain and polyurethane well.

name: White Oak
finish_recommendation: Oil finishes and polyurethane both work well. Tannins can react with water-based finishes — test first.

name: Hard Maple
finish_recommendation: Resists stain absorption — best left natural or with a clear finish like oil or lacquer.

name: Black Walnut
finish_recommendation: Looks stunning with just an oil finish (tung or Danish oil). Avoid dark stains — the natural color is the appeal.

name: Cherry
finish_recommendation: Darkens beautifully with age and UV exposure. Use a light oil or shellac to enhance color. Avoid heavy stains.

name: White Ash
finish_recommendation: Takes stain evenly. Polyurethane or oil-based finishes work well. Can be ebonized effectively.

name: Poplar
finish_recommendation: Best painted — the greenish tint doesn't stain attractively. Excellent base for primer and paint.

name: Douglas Fir
finish_recommendation: Accepts stain but grain can be uneven. Exterior projects need a UV-protective finish or paint.

name: Eastern White Pine
finish_recommendation: Prone to blotching — always use a pre-conditioner before staining. Paint or clear wax work reliably.

name: Teak
finish_recommendation: High natural oil content — wipe with naphtha before finishing. Teak oil or leave unfinished outdoors. Glue joints need degreasing first.

name: Mahogany (Honduran)
finish_recommendation: Takes stain and finish beautifully. Oil, lacquer, or varnish all work well. A grain filler improves smoothness.

name: Hickory
finish_recommendation: Very hard — sand thoroughly before finishing. Oil-based polyurethane recommended for durability.

name: Cedar (Western Red)
finish_recommendation: Naturally rot-resistant. Leave unfinished outdoors (weathers to silver-grey) or use a clear exterior sealer to preserve color.

name: Birch
finish_recommendation: Similar to maple — resists stain evenly. Best with clear finishes or paint. Pre-conditioner helps if staining.

name: Purple Heart
finish_recommendation: Vivid purple color fades to brown with UV exposure. Use a UV-inhibiting finish to preserve color. Oil finishes alone are not enough.

name: Basswood
finish_recommendation: Very easy to finish. Paint, stain, or clear coat all work well. Pre-conditioner helps with even stain absorption.
